input.content:
# Fix: Off-by-one error in `processItems`

## Context

Users report that the last item in the array passed to `processItems` in `src/utils.ts` is never processed. The root cause is a classic off-by-one error in the `for` loop condition: the loop terminates one iteration early, so the final element is always skipped. Four tests in `tests/utils.test.ts` currently fail because of this.

---

## Critical Files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/utils.ts` | Contains the buggy `processItems` function |
| `tests/utils.test.ts` | Tests that exercise `processItems` and verify the fix |

---

## The Bug

**File:** `src/utils.ts`, line 7

```typescript
// BUGGY — stops one index before the last element
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {
```

For an array of length N (indices 0 … N-1), the condition `i < items.length - 1` only evaluates to true for indices 0 … N-2, silently dropping the last element every time.

---

## Fix (single-line change)

**File:** `src/utils.ts`, line 7

```typescript
// FIXED — iterates over every element
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
```

Remove the `- 1` from the loop condition. No other code needs to change.

---

## Failing Tests (all fixed by the one-line change)

| Test | Input | Expected | Actual (buggy) |
|---|---|---|---|
| should process all items | `["hello","world","foo"]` | `["HELLO","WORLD","FOO"]` | `["HELLO","WORLD"]` |
| should handle a single item | `["test"]` | `["TEST"]` | `[]` |
| should handle two items | `["a","b"]` | `["A","B"]` | `["A"]` |
| should return empty array | `[]` | `[]` | `[]` ✓ |

---

## Verification

1. Apply the one-line fix to `src/utils.ts`.
2. Run the test suite: `npm test` (uses Vitest).
3. All 6 tests (`processItems` × 4 + `sum` × 2) should pass with no failures.

content.text:
## Plan Summary

**Root cause:** Off-by-one error in `src/utils.ts`, line 7.

```typescript
// BUGGY
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {

// FIXED
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
```

The `- 1` in the loop condition causes the loop to stop one index short, so the last element is always skipped. Removing it fixes the bug entirely.

**Impact of the bug:**
| Input | Expected | Actual (buggy) |
|---|---|---|
| `["hello","world","foo"]` | `["HELLO","WORLD","FOO"]` | `["HELLO","WORLD"]` |
| `["test"]` | `["TEST"]` | `[]` |
| `["a","b"]` | `["A","B"]` | `["A"]` |
| `[]` | `[]` | `[]` ✓ |

**Verification:** Run `npm test` — all 6 tests (4 for `processItems`, 2 for `sum`) should pass after the single-character fix.
